response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with a strong web/cloud application background, but the role requires deep hardware expertise — specifically SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in this resume. The candidate's closest hardware-adjacent work is C/C++ tooling at National Instruments and low-latency software at Dunnhumby, but these are software engineering roles, not hardware architecture positions. There is no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, deep learning workload optimization, or silicon-level performance modeling.
strengths: 17+ years of software engineering experience, meeting the BS+8yr education/experience threshold on paper
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second, showing some performance engineering aptitude
C/C++ experience at National Instruments on an engineering IDE, providing minor hardware-adjacent context
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience whatsoever — the primary technical requirement of the role
No AI, machine learning, or deep learning workload experience of any kind
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design background; career is entirely software-layer focused
